message:
perf: enable jemalloc by default, optimize release for speed, cache regexes, and reuse fuzzy normalizations

Build configuration:
- Enable jemalloc in default feature set (was opt-in only), reducing
  heap fragmentation and improving allocation throughput for long-running
  agent sessions
- Change release opt-level from "z" (minimize size) to 3 (maximize
  speed) — binary size budget is still enforced by the perf gate at
  22 MB but runtime throughput benefits significantly

Performance constants:
- Add BINARY_SIZE_RELEASE_BUDGET_MB (22.0) in perf_build.rs as the
  single source of truth, replacing magic numbers in perf_budgets.rs
  and perf_regression.rs

Regex caching (resources.rs):
- Cache positional-arg ($1, $2, …) and slice-arg (${@:start:length})
  regexes using OnceLock, eliminating per-call Regex::new() overhead
  during resource argument substitution
- Refactor replace_regex() to accept &regex::Regex instead of &str
  pattern, making the caching explicit and removing the internal
  unwrap_or fallback

Fuzzy text matching (tools.rs):
- Add fuzzy_find_text_with_normalized() variant that accepts optional
  pre-computed normalized strings and returns them for reuse
- In EditTool, pre-compute normalized content + old_text once and pass
  through both the match and occurrence-counting paths, avoiding 2x
  redundant O(n) normalization on every edit operation

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
